exampleSentences: Bầu trời đang nắng, bỗng dưng mưa to làm anh không mang ô.
Chị đang làm việc thì bỗng dưng điện thoại reo rất lớn.
Em cảm thấy bỗng dưng rất buồn mà không rõ lý do.
notes:
Bỗng dưng means suddenly or all of a sudden. It often carries a slight feeling of surprise or “out of nowhere.”

It’s an adverb, usually placed before the verb or before the whole clause.  

It can also suggest something happens without a clear reason, similar to “for no reason.”  
Close words: đột nhiên (more neutral/formal “suddenly”), tự nhiên (can mean “naturally” or “suddenly,” so be careful).

content.sentence: Cô ấy ___ cười khi thấy tôi.

translation.en: She suddenly laughed when she saw me.

notes_md:
**Bỗng dưng** means *suddenly* or *all of a sudden*. It often carries a slight feeling of surprise or “out of nowhere.”

It’s an adverb, usually placed before the verb or before the whole clause.  

It can also suggest something happens without a clear reason, similar to “for no reason.”  
Close words: **đột nhiên** (more neutral/formal “suddenly”), **tự nhiên** (can mean “naturally” or “suddenly,” so be careful).
exampleSentencesTranslated: The sky was sunny, but suddenly it rained heavily and I didn't bring an umbrella.
She was working when suddenly the phone rang very loudly.
I suddenly felt very sad and didn't know why.

definitions.vi: Một cách bất ngờ hoặc đột ngột.
